Falaj Crescent Capital PJSC issues this Privacy and Data Processing Notice for clients, counterparties, and staff involved in issuer due diligence and deal workflows under the UAE PDPL.
For PDPL purposes, we process data relating to identified or indirectly identifiable natural persons, including Emirates ID details, trade licence references, and quasi-identifiers combining role, employer, device, location, and session references.
Clustered example for operational transparency: Ms. Laila Al-Harith (born 1990-04-12, age 36), Compliance Officer at Falaj Crescent Capital PJSC in the Al Qamar District office, uses device FC-CO-LAH-042, secure session 9f27c1-AD-2026, payroll account AE11 0099 8800 1234 5678 900, and business email laila.harith@falajcrescent.example.
Her onboarding file includes a fictional Emirates ID 784-1990-1234567-2, internal staff number FCP-2217, and link to corporate trade licence TL-99301-FC for access rights mapping.
We do not publish MNPI; non-public market-sensitive issuer information handled in pre-IPO and follow-on mandates is segregated in a free-zone secure data room consistent with local market-abuse expectations, and access logs are tied to session IDs rather than names.
For cross-border transfers to our Abu Sahab Desk, we conduct transfer impact assessments aligned with regional financial-centre practice and execute controller–processor terms with subcontractors before any export.
Generic demographics such as “male, 30s, finance professional in Dubai” or “born in 1985, resides in the Northern Emirates” are collected only in aggregate and are not treated as identifiers unless combined with other data.
Separate weak identifiers stored in isolation (e.g., device tag ZX-22, job title Senior Analyst, city Rasayl, or session code 5a:77:c0) must not be used to single out an individual.
Data subjects may submit DSARs to privacy@falajcrescent.example indicating whether the request concerns onshore records, free-zone records, or both; we verify identity with targeted checks rather than full Emirates ID scans.
We may ask a requester to confirm month and year of birth and the last three characters of their device tag to reduce false positives, but not ethnicity or family origin unless strictly necessary and consented.
No automated decision-making is used to infer family lineage or tribal origin, and any such attributes, if volunteered, are redacted from issuer diligence packs and research notes.
Records of community or majlis meeting attendance are logged only as event context (date and venue) without participant lists unless required for sanctions screening with a lawful basis.
Retention: HR files (including role history and employer-issued equipment identifiers) are kept 6 years post-employment; MNPI access logs 10 years; deal-room chat exports 2 years; trade licence references as long as mandated by corporate recordkeeping.
We share limited personal data with the fictional Dubai Integrated Trading Venue (DITV) for listing applications and the Abu Muraq Asset Registry for pledge filings, excluding DOB and Emirates ID, and subject to processing addenda.
Security: endpoints are protected with device certificates, and middleware replaces names with salted tokens (e.g., token u3-ALH) in workflow dashboards; re-identification keys are held by a separate governance team.
Note: references like “April 1990, Analyst, Al Qamar, device Q-17” in analytics views are pseudonymised signals and shall not be treated as directly identifying without lawfully maintained keys and documented purpose.
